title: WILDFIRE ALERT: Forest Fires Spread Across J&K, Himachal & Uttarakhand Amid Rising Heatwave - ABP News
full_text:
WILDFIRE ALERT: Forest Fires Spread Across J&K, Himachal & Uttarakhand Amid Rising Heatwave
Widespread forest fires have engulfed several parts of North India, with Jammu & Kashmir, Himachal Pradesh, and Uttarakhand witnessing severe wildfire incidents amid rising temperatures and dry weather conditions. The situation has become particularly alarming as flames continue to spread across forested regions, threatening both ecology and nearby settlements. In Himachal Pradesh, forests around Shimla, Solan, Kasol, and Kotkhai are heavily affected, with thick smoke blanketing hill slopes and reducing visibility in several areas. Authorities report that the fire has spread rapidly due to strong winds and dry vegetation, making containment efforts difficult. In Jammu & Kashmir, districts such as Rajouri, Udhampur, and Ramban have reported extensive forest fires covering large stretches of woodland. Officials have described the situation as serious, noting that steep terrain and lack of direct accessibility are hampering firefighting operations. Local residents, along with forest department teams, are attempting to control the blaze using limited resources. Uttarakhand is also witnessing significant wildfire activity, with regions including Rudraprayag, Nainital, Dehradun, Ranikhet, Tehri Garhwal, Almora, Chamoli, Uttarkashi, and Lansdowne reporting multiple fire outbreaks. The fires have damaged large forest areas, raising environmental concerns and adding pressure on local disaster management teams. Authorities highlight that firefighting in hilly terrain remains extremely challenging due to limited access, lack of water supply infrastructure, and rapid wind-driven spread of flames. Continuous monitoring and emergency response efforts are underway across affected districts. Experts warn that rising temperatures and prolonged dry spells are contributing to the increasing frequency and intensity of forest fires, adding to environmental degradation and accelerating local warming conditions.
